channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula confirma que Geraldo Alckmin será candidato a vice na chapa que disputará reeleição
item.description: Lula confirmed that Geraldo Alckmin will be the vice-presidential candidate on his re-election ticket in 2026. Alckmin must resign from his position as Minister of Development, Industry, and Trade by April 4, as required by electoral law, to be eligible to run. The announcement was made during a ministerial meeting, on the same day that at least 14 ministers declared their departure from government to run in the October elections.

item.title: Em reunião ministerial, Lula diz que 'política piorou muito', virou negócio e cargos têm 'preço alto'
item.description: During a ministerial meeting at the Planalto Palace, President Lula stated that politics has 'gotten much worse', turned into a 'business', and that public offices now carry a 'very high price'. He said everyone shares 'some blame' for the decline in politics and institutions, but believes the situation can be fixed through politics — provided the people are convinced they can change the political landscape they disagree with. The remarks come as at least 14 ministers prepare to leave government to run in the October elections, following the end of the incompatibilization period on April 4.

item.title: CPI do Crime Organizado aprova convocação de ex-governadores Cláudio Castro e Ibaneis Rocha
item.description: The Organized Crime Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPI) approved the summoning of former governors Cláudio Castro (RJ) and Ibaneis Rocha (DF) to investigate the Master case, involving suspicions of money laundering and use of financial institutions to conceal illicit funds. Castro resigned one day before being convicted by the TSE for abuse of power, while Ibaneis stepped down to run for Senate in 2026. The summons aim to determine whether authorities interfered with or neglected oversight of suspicious financial operations.

item.title: A pedido de Lula, Fazenda negocia com bancos medidas para reduzir endividamento das famílias
item.description: At Lula's request, the Ministry of Finance is negotiating with banks to reduce household and corporate indebtedness, particularly targeting non-delinquent debt burden on family income, to ease household budgets in the election year. The government is also considering postponing electricity tariff increases by providing credit to distributors, deferring the cost to next year.

item.title: Eleições 2026: aberto credenciamento para Missões de Observação Eleitoral Nacional
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) has opened accreditation for National Electoral Observation Missions (MOE) for the 2026 General Elections. Civil society organizations, higher education institutions (public or private), and other entities can register until July 5, 2026, via the TSE's electronic system. Observation activities may continue until the inauguration of elected officials, with individual observers required to be accredited within 30 days of mission approval or by August 21, 2026 — whichever comes first.

item.title: Qualquer candidato que for ao segundo turno vai ganhar do Lula, diz Caiado a rádio
item.description: The governor of Goiás and PSD presidential pre-candidate, Ronaldo Caiado, stated in an interview with Rádio CBN that any candidate who reaches the second round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election will defeat current President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. He expressed confidence in the opposition's ability to unite and criticized the current government, positioning himself as a viable alternative for a runoff.

item.title: Interrogatório de Eduardo Bolsonaro é marcado para abril; deputado não apresentou defesa e está nos EUA
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes has scheduled Eduardo Bolsonaro's interrogation for April 14 via videoconference, after the deputy, residing in the U.S., failed to present a defense following official citation. He is accused of attempting to interfere with the trial of the coup plot that convicted his father, former President Jair Bolsonaro, constituting the crime of coercion in the course of proceedings. Eduardo lost his congressional seat due to absences and is under administrative investigation by the Federal Police.

item.title: 'Polarização está chegando em um grau insustentável', diz Ronaldo Caiado em entrevista
item.description: Goiás Governor and PSD presidential pre-candidate Ronaldo Caiado warns that political polarization in Brazil is reaching an unsustainable level, advocating for governance through peace-building rather than confrontation. He cites his 88% approval rating in Goiás as evidence that effective governance renders the PT irrelevant in the state, positioning himself as the candidate capable of defeating Lula in a runoff. Caiado differentiates himself from Flávio Bolsonaro by emphasizing moral authority and substantive debate, likening the presidency to choosing a competent doctor for one’s child based on skill and results, not past failures.

item.title: Caiado se apresenta ao eleitor como Frankenstein ideológico
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado positioned himself before voters using the metaphor of 'the captain’s vintage wine,' alluding to bolsonarism as an exhausted ideological inheritance. The text critiques this stance as a forced attempt to portray himself as the heir of an authoritarian right, likening it to an 'ideological Frankenstein' — artificially assembled and lacking coherence. It suggests that while the dictatorship favored military candidates by objective criteria (like ranks), today’s political legitimacy is more subjective, and Caiado fails to build a consistent identity.

item.title: A aposta jurídica de Castro para reverter a inelegibilidade e concorrer ao Senado
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), declared ineligible by the TSE for abuse of power in the 2022 elections, is appealing the decision, arguing that the lack of unanimity in both the TSE (5-2) and TRE (4 favorable votes) rulings undermines the ineligibility. He claims that leaving the governorship reduces political pressure and that ineligibility cannot be sustained without unanimous judicial consensus. His legal bet hinges on the absence of effective personal benefit, as noted in two favorable TSE votes.

item.title: RS e SE entram em programa de subsídio para baratear diesel
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul and Sergipe announced their adherence to the federal diesel import subsidy program, offering a discount of R$1.20 per liter, equally funded by the Union and the states (R$0.60 each). The measure is exceptional, temporary, and limited to up to two months, aiming to ensure fuel supply predictability and ease fiscal pressures, particularly in RS amid post-climate tragedy reconstruction. São Paulo may join, with an estimated impact of R$3.2 billion (R$1.6 billion from the Union and R$1.6 billion from the states).

item.title: Dívida sequestra sensação de bem-estar e vira preocupação central do governo em ano eleitoral
item.description: Despite positive economic indicators such as low unemployment and controlled inflation, high household debt levels in Brazil are undermining public well-being and becoming a central concern for the government in an election year. According to the latest Quaest poll, 48% of Brazilians believe the economy worsened over the past year, while only 24% saw improvement, highlighting a disconnect between macroeconomic data and public sentiment. The government is seeking solutions to alleviate debt’s impact on consumption and electoral perception.

item.title: Eduardo Leite vai apoiar Caiado?
item.description: Snubbed by the PSD, Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite has not yet declared his support for Ronaldo Caiado's presidential bid. According to O Globo, Leite has told allies he is not in a rush to decide on his position for the 2026 elections.

item.title: Caiado disputa com Flávio Bolsonaro o posto de candidato antiLula
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, PSD presidential candidate, aims to position himself as the main anti-Lula contender, challenging Flávio Bolsonaro of the PL, who leads opposition preferences. Caiado emphasizes his long political trajectory, including two terms as governor of Goiás and high state approval ratings, to contrast with Bolsonaro’s alleged inexperience in public security. With only about three months until the party convention, Caiado must overcome his poor electoral showing in 1989 to be seen as viable.

item.title: Artigo: Eleições diretas para governador no Rio e o valor da democracia
item.description: The article discusses the political crisis in Rio de Janeiro following the resignation of Governor Cláudio Castro and the annulment of his mandate by the TSE, emphasizing that resignation does not terminate the impeachment process nor prevent sanctions such as ineligibility. The governor's office is currently held by the president of the TJ-RJ, Justice Ricardo Couto, and the legal debate centers on whether elections for the position should be direct or indirect. The editorial argues that direct elections are the response recommended by law and jurisprudence.

item.title: Maioria das políticas para criança e adolescente avançou nos últimos anos
item.description: Between January 2023 and July 2025, the Agenda 227 monitoring found that 14.8% of the 81 targets for children and adolescents were fully met and 37% showed significant progress, with only 8.6% showing no evolution or regression. Health and nutrition were the areas with the greatest advances, highlighting the Cuida Mais Brazil Program, the Alyne Network, and the recovery of vaccination coverage through the National Vaccination Movement. In contrast, efforts to address violence and public budget allocation continued to face persistent challenges.

item.title: Lula já sabe qual faixa do eleitorado precisa reconquistar para melhorar nas pesquisas
item.description: Lula has identified that he needs to win back younger voters to improve his polling performance, as rejection among this group has risen, surprising his allies given their historical alignment with progressive candidates. He plans to make targeted gestures to this demographic to broaden his support, especially amid a technical tie with Flávio Bolsonaro in recent polls.

item.title: Pesquisa mostra que Flávio Bolsonaro ainda é pouco conhecido do eleitorado brasileiro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro remains largely unknown to the Brazilian electorate, with only 36.8% of respondents saying they 'know him well,' while 54.5% say they have only heard of him. Despite low name recognition, he already faces substantial rejection, with 44.1% of voters stating they would never vote for him for president.

item.title: Paraná Pesquisas: 54,5% conhecem Flávio “apenas de ouvir falar”
item.description: A Paraná Pesquisas survey reveals that 54.5% of Brazilians know PL pre-candidate and senator Flávio Bolsonaro only by hearsay. Another 36.8% say they know him well, while 8.7% claim not to know him at all. The share of respondents saying they do not know him has decreased compared to earlier polls.

item.title: ‘PF não protege nem persegue. A gente trabalha com isenção’, diz Diretor-geral da corporação
item.description: The director-general of the Federal Police, Andrei Rodrigues, stated that the agency operates with impartiality, neither protecting nor persecuting anyone for political reasons, during an event celebrating the PF's 82nd anniversary. He emphasized that ongoing investigations, including those into Banco Master and the bank secrecy waiver for Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, are free from ideological or political influence. Andrei also praised the Central Bank and Coaf for their work, despite recent criticisms and STF-imposed restrictions on financial intelligence reports.

item.title: 47,0% rejeitam votar em Lula e 44,1%, em Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: A Paraná Pesquisas survey reveals that 47% of voters familiar with Lula say they would not vote for him under any circumstances in the 2026 presidential election, compared to 30.4% who would definitely vote for him and 21.4% who might consider it. Flávio Bolsonaro faces 44.1% rejection among those who know him, while 54.7% express favorability, with 30.1% certain to vote for him and 24.6% open to supporting him. Lula is known by 98.9% of voters, whereas Flávio Bolsonaro is recognized by 91.3%.

item.title: A disputa cada vez mais acirrada entre Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro, segundo pesquisa
item.description: A Paraná Pesquisas survey shows a technical tie between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in 2026 presidential vote intentions, with 30.4% for the incumbent president and 30.1% for the senator. Lula maintains a lead in public recognition (83.3% vs 36.3%), while Flávio has grown since January, rising from 26.3% to 30.1%. Rejection is higher against Lula (47%) than against Flávio (44.1%).

item.title: Valdemar defende vice mulher para Flávio, empodera Michelle e abre divergência no clã Bolsonaro
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL, advocated for Flávio Bolsonaro to select a woman as his running mate in the 2026 presidential race, arguing it would boost female voter engagement due to Michelle Bolsonaro’s work with PL Mulher. The proposal caused tension within the Bolsonaro clan, as it would give the former first lady an active role in shaping the ticket — a move resisted by her sons, Flávio and Eduardo Bolsonaro, particularly if the chosen woman is not Senator Tereza Cistina.

item.title: Série Panama Papers desvendou empresas offshore e completa 10 anos
item.description: The Panama Papers series marks 10 years since its 2016 publication, exposing how politicians and businesspeople worldwide used offshore companies in tax havens to move funds with low transparency. Led by the ICIJ with 376 journalists from 76 countries, the investigation analyzed 2.6 terabytes of leaked documents from Mossack Fonseca, including Brazilian outlet Poder360, which uncovered offshore links involving contractors, lobbyists, and politicians across the ideological spectrum.

item.title: Corte de possíveis fraudes no INSS sob Lula cai de 92% para 15%
item.description: Dataprev's effectiveness in cutting fraudulent associative discounts in the INSS dropped from 92% in 2022 to 15% in 2025 under Lula's administration, while exclusion requests surged from 334,000 to 4.4 million. The company blames the rise in unprocessed requests on faulty data from associations, such as requests to cancel non-existent discounts or invalid benefit numbers. The data was part of the INSS CPI's analysis, which concluded its work in March 2026.

item.title: Eleições 2026: quem são os pré-candidatos à Presidência da República
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás, was announced as the PSD's pre-candidate for president, defeating internal rival Eduardo Leite, while Ratinho Junior withdrew his candidacy. Lula (PT) is seeking re-election for an unprecedented fourth term, leading in first-round polls, while Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) is his main opponent, tied with Lula in second-round scenarios according to recent surveys. Both remain pre-candidates, awaiting party conventions and official registration with the TSE in August.

item.title: Plano Clima (Mitigação e Adaptação) aprovado
item.description: Resolution CIM/CC/PR 09/26, approved on June 24, 2026, establishes the Mitigation and Adaptation Climate Plans with National Strategies and Sectoral Plans, marking a significant advance in Brazil's climate governance by setting diligence standards for public and private actors through 2035. This measure consolidates the foundation laid during Lula's second term (2007-2010) with the creation of the CIM and the National Climate Change Plan, overcoming periods of policy setbacks and strengthening the national legal framework for climate action.

item.title: RJ: TRE inicia retotalização para definir substituto de Bacellar na Alerj
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Electoral Court (TRE) has begun the vote recount for the 2022 state deputy elections following the annulment of Rodrigo Bacellar's mandate by the TSE due to fraud linked to the Ceperj case. The process involves nullifying 97,822 votes obtained by Bacellar, recalculating electoral and party quotients, and determining which candidates are entitled to the seat in Alerj. Parties will have three days to review the figures and two days to file objections before the TRE plenary makes a final decision.

item.title: Caiado é ignorado por maioria do PSD no Nordeste, que dará palanque a Lula
item.description: Most of the PSD in the Northeast has decided to support Lula's re-election, ignoring the candidacy of Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás, who was announced as the party's presidential candidate. The decision reflects the party's historical closeness to the PT and Lula in the region, prioritizing local alliances over the party's own national candidacy.

item.title: Dança das cadeiras dos ministros de Lula: veja quem sai do governo para ser candidato
item.description: At least 17 of Lula’s 38 ministers will leave office to run in the October elections, amid declining poll numbers. The departures comply with electoral law requiring officials to resign by April 4. Vice President Geraldo Alckmin will step down from the MDIC but remain on Lula’s ticket, targeting conservative voters in São Paulo and supporting Fernando Haddad’s gubernatorial bid.

item.title: Atlas/Estadão: Lula é desaprovado por 56% dos paulistas e aprovado por 43%
item.description: The Atlas/Estadão poll shows 56% of São Paulo residents disapprove of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, while 43% approve, with a 2-point margin of error. Disapproval is particularly high among men (61.4%), evangelicals (80.4%), voters aged 45–59 (70.6%), and those who voted for Tarcísio de Freitas in 2022 (98.4%). Lula’s government is rated as poor or terrible by 55% of respondents.

item.title: Lula mantém promessa eleitoral de tarifa zero, mas sem previsão orçamentária para tirar do papel
item.description: The Lula government maintains the proposal for zero-fare public transportation as an electoral promise for 2026, but acknowledges there is no budget forecast to implement it. Estimated annual costs range from R$90 billion to R$200 billion, and the initiative is seen as 'impossible' by presidential allies due to lack of fiscal space and time in an election year. Deputy Jilmar Tatto (SP) is drafting the government program section on the issue, despite 108 similar bills currently under consideration in Congress.

item.title: No STM, Garnier insiste em audiências com atual comandante da Marinha e Aldo Rebelo
item.description: The defense of Almir Garnier, former Navy commander convicted of participating in the post-2022 election coup attempt, has requested the Superior Military Tribunal (STM) to produce new evidence and conduct diligences before judgment on a case that could declare his indignity for official status. The request includes hearings with the current Navy commander and Aldo Rebelo, former minister and close figure to ex-president Bolsonaro. The article is subscriber-only and requires registration for full access.

item.title: A resposta de Eduardo Leite sobre apoiar ou não Ronaldo Caiado para presidente
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite does not intend to immediately announce his support for Ronaldo Caiado, despite being sidelined by the PSD leadership in the party's presidential candidate selection. His stance contrasts with that of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior, who endorsed Caiado after withdrawing from the internal race. The article highlights internal PSD divisions over support for the party's chosen candidate for the 2026 election.

item.title: Erika Hilton chama pastores anti-LGBTQIA+ de “cafetões da fé”
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (Psol-SP) labeled religious leaders opposing LGBTQIA+ agendas as "pimps of faith," accusing them of profiting from people's fear, hope, and religiosity, citing cases like Banco Master and Clava Forte Bank tied to the Lagoinha Baptist Church. She criticized judicial and legislative bodies for failing to enforce the STF ruling that equates LGBTphobia with racism, describing their inaction as omission and contempt. Hilton urged society to awaken to the manipulation of faith for enrichment and hate, insisting the Gospel promotes love, not war or discrimination.

item.title: Moraes marca interrogatório de Eduardo Bolsonaro para 14 de abril
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has scheduled Eduardo Bolsonaro's interrogation for April 14 in a case where he is accused of coercion related to the attempted coup. Bolsonaro, residing in the U.S. since February 2025, will be questioned via videoconference starting at 2 p.m. He was indicted by the PGR in September 2025 for allegedly pressuring Brazilian authorities using U.S. sanctions to benefit his father, former president Jair Bolsonaro.

item.title: Golpismo da direita permanece no centro da disputa eleitoral
item.description: Right-wing golpism remains the central problem in Brazilian elections, repeating the pattern from the two previous presidential disputes. Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, following his father's example, again showed institutional disrespect by questioning the integrity of the electoral process before a foreign audience, without condemnation from other right-wing forces. The article argues that, 62 years after the military coup, Brazil is once again facing a democratic crisis.

item.title: Caiado na pista
item.description: The selection of Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as the PSD's presidential candidate aims to provide an alternative to the polarization between Lula supporters and Bolsonaristas, capitalizing on voter fatigue with the ongoing dispute since 2018. While Caiado is not a complete novelty in politics, his experience in public office positions him as a fresh and credible factor in the electoral race.

item.title: Alta do querosene de aviação pressiona governo a acelerar medidas para conter aumento no preço das passagens
item.description: The Lula government is seeking to accelerate measures to mitigate the impact of rising aviation kerosene (QAV) prices, which Vibra Energia increased by 54.63% effective April 1, pressuring airlines and threatening higher airfares. Proposed actions include zeroing the IOF on airlines, reducing PIS/Cofins on QAV and income tax on aircraft leasing, aiming to correct asymmetries from the sector's exposure to international shocks. Despite being a major producer, Brazil imports 20% of its QAV demand, and the fuel accounts for roughly 30% of airline costs in an industry with global margins below 4%.

item.title: Endividamento: veja o que o governo negocia com os bancos para aliviar orçamento das famílias
item.description: The federal government proposed to banks a new debt renegotiation program to ease household budgets, as family indebtedness reached 49.7% of annual income and 29.3% of monthly commitments. Unlike the Desenrola program, this initiative aims to be faster and may include government incentives to improve client conditions, amid concerns over high credit interest rates, especially credit card revolving debt at 435.9% per year.

item.title: Em meio à indefinição sobre novo governador, estado tem desafios e prazos a cumprir; entenda
item.description: Rio de Janeiro faces uncertainty over its next governor after Claudio Castro's resignation and Vice Governor Thiago Pampolha's departure, with TJ-RJ President Ricardo Couto serving as interim governor. The STF will rule on April 8 whether to hold direct or indirect elections to fill the mandate until December 31, while the PL seeks to install the future Alerj president as governor, pending a recount of Rodrigo Bacellar's votes. Key challenges include reclaiming territories from drug gangs and militias, joining the Propag debt program, and releasing emergency health funds to the city.

item.title: Câmara trava na volta do recesso e fica sem votações em quase metade dos dias úteis
item.description: The Chamber of Deputies held votes on only 21 of 39 business days between February 2 and the end of March 2026, reflecting a recurring pattern of low legislative productivity observed since 2023, regardless of the chamber's presidency. This slowdown is influenced by Carnival, the entrenched use of semi-remote sessions, and the proximity of the 2026 presidential elections, although significant bills such as the Anti-Faction Bill and the Public Security PEC were approved.

item.title: Caiado mira em Lula ao assumir candidatura e testa discurso para não ser visto como 'linha auxiliar' de Flávio
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado has been confirmed as the PSD's presidential candidate for 2026, aiming to position himself as an independent alternative by directly confronting Lula while avoiding alignment with Flávio Bolsonaro. His strategy centers on criticizing Lula's government on public safety and the economy, leveraging his record in Goiás, while rejecting bolsonarist ties to appeal to centrist and moderate right-wing voters.

item.title: STF julga eleição do Rio dia 8, e PL pede que eleito na Alerj assuma governo
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court (STF) will rule on April 8 on whether Rio de Janeiro will hold a direct or indirect election for an interim governor to serve until year-end. The PL party has requested amicus curiae status, arguing that the newly elected president of the State Legislative Assembly (Alerj) should automatically assume the interim governorship under the traditional succession line, regardless of the STF’s final decision on the election model. The move aims to enable Douglas Ruas, PL’s pre-candidate and favorite to lead Alerj, to become interim governor before the interim election takes place.

item.title: Caiado ataca PT, critica falta de 'experiência' de Flávio Bolsonaro e promete anistia para tentar virar voto
item.description: Governor Ronaldo Caiado of Goiás was confirmed as the PSD's pre-candidate for the 2026 presidency, promising to deactivate national polarization. In his first speech, he criticized the PT and questioned Flávio Bolsonaro's experience for inter-branch dialogue, while announcing he would grant amnesty to those convicted in the January 8 events, including former president Jair Bolsonaro, as a pacification measure.

item.title: Eduardo chama Doria de “bosta” e diz que ele abriu caminho para PT
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro attacked João Doria on social media, calling him 'shit' and accusing him of paving the way for the PT's return to power, criticizing his pandemic management and pro-vaccination stance. Doria, speaking at a Lide event in São Paulo, claimed Bolsonaro would have been reelected had he supported vaccination, receiving public affirmation from Valdemar Costa Neto. The exchange highlights the deep polarization among Bolsonarist allies and former opponents regarding pandemic policies and their political aftermath.

item.title: Caiado ataca de Lula a Flávio Bolsonaro e projeta racha na direita
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), launched his pre-candidacy for President of the Republic with criticism of the Lula government and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), questioning his administrative experience. Caiado sought to position himself as an alternative on the right, stating that defeating the PT at the ballot box is easy, but the real challenge is governing so that the PT is no longer a viable option in the country. He emphasized the importance of political experience and institutional articulation as essential for governing, while attempting to attract centrist voters and contest the right-wing space currently dominated by Bolsonarism.

item.title: ACM Neto define vice e lança chapa para disputar o governo da Bahia
item.description: ACM Neto, the União Brasil pre-candidate for governor of Bahia, officially launched his 2026 election ticket at the CDL theater in Feira de Santana. The slate includes Jequié mayor Zé Cocá (PP) as vice-governor and senatorial candidates João Roma (PL) and Angelo Coronel (Republicanos).

item.title: Governo deve publicar MP para conter alta do diesel mesmo sem adesão total dos estados; veja os pontos
item.description: The Lula government plans to issue a provisional measure to subsidize imported diesel at R$ 1.20 per liter, with costs split equally between the Union and states, for two months, even without full state adherence. The estimated fiscal impact is R$ 1.5 billion per month. São Paulo and Sergipe have confirmed adherence, while other states are negotiating, with expectations of an 'expressive majority'.

item.title: Vibra reajusta em 54% querosene de aviação por alta do petróleo
item.description: Vibra Energia announced a 54.63% increase in aviation kerosene prices, effective April 1, 2026, driven by rising oil prices due to geopolitical tensions, including U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran that disrupted the Strait of Hormuz. The move comes amid criticism from President Lula’s government over the 2021 privatization of BR Distribuidora, with the PT pushing to reestablish state control through a parliamentary front. Although the PT aimed to launch the initiative by March 24, it failed to gather sufficient support.
